_note: docs-studio (failure by vacancy: hollow components + dead-but-wired MCP).
_provenance: RE-SOURCED from real council transcripts (I-13 deeper fix): each phrasing below is a wording the council ACTUALLY produced in runs/2026-06-10-docs-studio-run1.md / run2.md / run3.md — lightly trimmed of bylines/scaffolding, not author-invented. So a green check-recall now means 'the checker's patterns still catch wordings the council really used' (a measurement) rather than 'the patterns match the author's paraphrase of its own patterns' (the closed loop). Critic identities stay obscured as in the source runs.

H hollow components — bodies thinner tha: the description is the entire knowledge, the body is empty; every session pays for two verbose descriptions that route to nothing
the post-install surface over-promises and a user can't tell from plugin details that the bodies are empty stubs
the skills' depth claims are unfalsifiable against the bundle — no references/, no rule list, no fixtures
the skills are empty shells whose descriptions promise depth that isn't on disk
doc-rules/style-canon one-sentence bodies under comprehensive-catalog descriptions, no references/
L dead-but-wired MCP — defines tools but: a contract with no implementation — the inverse of the API-wrapper anti-pattern, and worse; no stdio read loop, no initialize/tools/list/tools/call dispatch
a liveness check that the declared MCP server actually completes a JSON-RPC handshake
the MCP tool contract is a fiction: shapes without a server is a contract with no counterparty; no tools/call handler, no tools/list responder, no stdio loop
the bundled MCP is dead — docs-mcp.py self-documents 'no server loop to serve them' while .mcp.json launches it on enable
declared tools, no dispatch; tools/call apply_style cannot respond
no smoke test would catch the dead server
